daily_brief: The fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran has collapsed into renewed hostilities, with Iran's Revolutionary Guards claiming strikes against Jordan, Bahrain, Kuwait and Oman in retaliation for US attacks on Iranian territory [France 24, Financial Times, Globe and Mail]. The escalation threatens the 60-day interim agreement meant to establish a framework for permanent peace talks, with both sides disputing control of the Strait of Hormuz. In Paris, Ukraine's Western allies convened a 'Coalition of the Willing' meeting with at least 25 leaders seeking to secure additional air-defence commitments for Kyiv following a NATO summit aimed at demonstrating transatlantic unity [France 24, Le Monde]. US Republican Senator Lindsey Graham died at age 71 from an aortic rupture, creating uncertainty in the narrowly-divided Senate where his absence further reduces the GOP's already precarious majority [BBC, Globe and Mail, RNZ]. A devastating fire at a Bangkok pub killed at least 27 people, with survivors describing how rapidly spreading smoke trapped patrons who fled toward bathrooms with no fire escapes [BBC, Globe and Mail, Le Monde]. Meanwhile, extreme heat continues to strain Europe, with a major wildfire burning through 800 hectares of Fontainebleau forest near Paris, prompting the unprecedented deployment of firefighting aircraft from southern France [BBC, RFI].

top_stories.headline: US-Iran Ceasefire Collapses as Tehran Strikes Gulf States in Retaliation for American Attacks
bullets: Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed strikes against Jordan, Bahrain, Kuwait and Oman in response to US airstrikes on Iranian territory, with Kuwait confirming its air defenses were firing Monday morning

The US military's Central Command reported hitting dozens of sites including air defence systems, radar installations, missile and drone equipment, with at least one person killed and seven injured in Isfahan province
Both sides are disputing control of the Strait of Hormuz, the critical waterway for global oil trade, with the escalation threatening the 60-day interim agreement framework for peace negotiations

The renewed fighting comes as both parties attempt to shape the terms of a memorandum of understanding, with Iran interpreting it as granting them leverage over the strait while the US and international community disagree

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes the threat to the interim agreement and includes EU energy task force assessment of jet fuel supplies

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Headline focuses on ceasefire teetering; limited content accessible

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of both sides' military actions and diplomatic context

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Includes analysis of internal Iranian politics and blame directed at President Pezeshkian

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Academic expert analysis suggesting Iran may have upper hand in test of wills

top_stories.headline: Ukraine's Western Allies Convene in Paris to Bolster Air Defence Support
bullets: At least 25 leaders joined Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky for a 'Coalition of the Willing' meeting focused on air and missile defence cooperation
The gathering follows a NATO summit aimed at demonstrating transatlantic unity and long-term support for Ukraine
Russia launched missile and drone attacks on Ukraine on Saturday, killing eight people and wounding dozens, underscoring Kyiv's vulnerability to ballistic missiles
Western allies are also working to develop a common position for potential talks with Russia and security guarantees for any eventual peace deal

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes air-defence shortages leaving Ukraine exposed despite battlefield momentum shifts

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article inaccessible but title confirms coverage of Paris rally for Ukraine support

top_stories.headline: US Senator Lindsey Graham Dies at 71; Preliminary Finding Points to Aortic Rupture
bullets: The South Carolina Republican died Saturday evening after what his office initially described as a 'brief and sudden illness'; medical examiner found aortic dissection related to hardening of arteries
Graham had just returned from Kyiv where he met President Zelensky on Friday and spoke with President Trump hours before his death
He served in Congress for over three decades, becoming one of Washington's most influential foreign policy voices and a close Trump ally after initially being a vocal critic
His death further reduces the already narrow Republican Senate majority, compounding challenges posed by Senator Mitch McConnell's extended absence

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Comprehensive biographical coverage including his evolution from Trump critic to ally

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Two articles: one on cause of death, another analyzing his political transformation and Senate implications

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Brief factual report on death and career highlights

top_stories.headline: Bangkok Bar Fire Kills at Least 27 as Lack of Fire Escapes Traps Patrons
bullets: Fire broke out near the stage of Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao pub in Bangkok's Chatuchak district just before midnight Sunday, spreading rapidly and cutting power
At least 27 people died and 22 remain in critical condition; most victims were found in a bathroom where they had sought shelter with no emergency exits available
Preliminary investigation suggests the fire may have been caused by an electrical short circuit in an air conditioner
Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul visited the scene; the incident highlights ongoing failures to enforce fire and electrical safety standards despite previous deadly fires

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Notes this is not the first such incident and that safety standards are poorly enforced despite official promises

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Detailed account of firefighter response and survivor accounts

top_stories.headline: Major Wildfire Burns Through 800 Hectares of Fontainebleau Forest Near Paris
bullets: Fire in the historic royal hunting forest about 60 kilometers southeast of Paris had burned through 800 hectares by Monday morning and was still spreading
Nearly 400 firefighters and two water bombing planes deployed; officials described the blaze as 'very virulent' and of 'exceptional scale'
First time firefighting aircraft have been sent from southern France to tackle fires in the Paris region, reflecting the severity of the third heatwave this year
The A6 highway, France's main north-south artery, was partially closed, causing significant travel disruption at the start of the Bastille Day holiday weekend

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasizes unprecedented nature of deploying southern firefighting aircraft to Paris region

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Details on evacuations, highway closures and firefighting coordination

additional_stories.headline: South Korean court sentences former President Yoon Suk Yeol to two years for receiving illegal polling services
Actor Sam Neill, star of Jurassic Park and The Piano, dies at 78 in Sydney
EU expert committee to deliver recommendations on regulating children's access to social media, including potential blanket ban
US Senator Mitch McConnell reveals extended absence was due to fall and mild pneumonia, says he was briefly unconscious
First patients enrolled in record-breaking Ebola treatment trial in DRC, just six weeks after WHO declared public health emergency
China rebukes New Zealand for supporting South China Sea arbitration ruling on 10th anniversary
At least 13 killed in Spanish wildfire near Bédar, including five believed to be British nationals
More than 2,700 deaths in England and Wales linked to record May and June heatwaves, researchers estimate
UK announces £250 million in funding to protect Jewish community following antisemitic attacks
Jannik Sinner retains Wimbledon title with four-set victory over Alexander Zverev
